Implied odds are limited here because I’m out of position and Elon has been barreling; raising as a semi‑bluff would bloat the pot against a very deep, aggressive opponent who is likely to call or shove, and I don’t want to commit more with only single‑card equity. I planned earlier to fold to heavy turn/river pressure when I miss — this is that spot. 

Decision: fold and preserve the stack for better‑priced opportunities.

I have pocket Aces on the button (SB) — the absolute premium hand heads-up. I want to build a pot and isolate the big blind while not overcommitting my relatively short 2690 stack against Elon's huge 37160. Raising to 400 total (~4x the BB) is a balanced size: it extracts value from Ax/Kx/pairs and charges speculative hands, but leaves enough behind to play post‑flop and to respond to 3‑bets. Plan: if Elon 3‑bets to a normal size I will call and continue for value on favorable flops; if he jams or 4‑bets to commit most of my stack I’ll shove for max value with the best starting hand. I will not limp or just call a single 50 — that gives too much free equity. Raise to 400 to seize initiative and maximize expected value.

98o is a weak offsuit hand with poor connectivity and dominated by many stronger hands in Minni's aggressive button raising range. Minni is short-stacked at ~23BB and raising to 4x from the button, indicating a wide range but still favoring live cards and playability that crush 98o. Pot odds are about 2.67:1 to call 300 into 600 (after call), but equity is insufficient (~30-35% vs typical button open), and postflop playability is bad OOP. As a deep stack (370+BB), folding preserves stack for better spots against this short stack who will shove wide but where I have equity advantage with premiums.

J4o is a very weak offsuit hand with poor playability and low equity against a calling range. In heads-up from the SB/Button, while ranges are wide, this is still a clear fold preflop against the BB, especially with a massive stack advantage (37k vs 2.7k). No need to risk chips or play out of position postflop with trash. Preserve stack for better opportunities with 31 hands remaining.

Heads-up at 50/100, I’m on the button (small blind) with 5♥8♥. In HU play I should be opening wide to take the initiative and use my postflop position. 5‑8 suited is well within a standard opening range. Calling 50 keeps the BB a free, wide range; raising gives fold equity and lets me play the hand aggressively on favorable flops. I’ll raise to a standard 3x the big blind (total 300) — big enough to discourage easy flats but small enough to leave a healthy stack behind (~2.5k) for postflop play. If Elon 3‑bets large I’ll re‑evaluate and likely fold given the hand and stack dynamics; if called I’ll C‑bet favorable textures and use position to realize equity.

Facing a button min-raise to 300 (call 200 into 400 pot) heads-up OOP with 6d Tc offsuit. This is a weak, uncoordinated hand with poor post-flop playability out of position against an aggressive opponent's wide opening range. Minni has been raising frequently from late position throughout the history, but 6To lacks the equity, blockers, or suitedness to justify defending. Pot odds are ~2:1, but implied odds are limited by villain's short stack (2590, ~26BB) and my deep stack (37k). Folding preserves stack for better spots in this late stage of the 150-hand game.

Pocket eights is a premium hand heads-up preflop, ahead of most of Minni's calling range. With position on the button/SB and Minni's stack at ~29BB, I raise to a standard 3x the BB (300 total) to build the pot, apply pressure, and realize equity. Folding or limping is suboptimal; this size balances value and bluff potential based on history where Minni defends wide but folds to aggression often.

I’m in the big blind with A♦6♦ facing a 300 open from the button. Heads-up the BTN opening range is wide and A6s is ahead of a lot of that range — it also has the Ace blocker and nut-draw potential (diamond). Pot is 400 and I must call 200 more to make it 300 total; that’s good odds to see a flop and keeps the pot manageable. A min 3-bet to 500 is an option to isolate, but I’m out of position postflop and don’t want to bloat the pot with a marginal A‑6s against a very deep and aggressive opponent. Plan: call, see the flop, play aggressively if I hit an Ace or diamond draw but otherwise control the pot and fold to excessive aggression.

Check. I called preflop to see a flop and the board (Td 8s Qc) leaves me with only Ace‑high (A♦6♦) and a weak runner‑runner diamond backdoor. I didn’t pair the Ace and I’m out of position against an aggressive BTN opener who often continues — betting here would bloat the pot with a marginal hand and expose me to frequent raises. Checking controls the pot, lets me realize equity cheaply, and keeps my options open: call small/medium barrel attempts to see a turn, fold to heavy pressure, and become aggressive only if I pair the Ace or pick up a real draw on the turn. Amount: 0 (check).

Flopped top set of eights on a Td8sQc rainbow board, which is very dry with no flush draws and limited straight draws (e.g., J9 gutshot). This is the nuts against Minni's BB defending range, which includes Qx, Tx, overpairs, and weaker pairs/draws from history of wide calls but folding to postflop aggression. She checked, indicating possible weakness. Betting for value to extract from worse hands like top pair, overpairs, or draws. Size: 450 (75% pot) balances protection against gutshots/implied odds while maximizing value from her ~27BB short stack without scaring off calls. Checking risks giving free cards or missing value.

Call — I’ll pay the 450 to see a turn (total committed = 300 preflop + 450 = 750). With A♦6♦ I have Ace‑high showdown value and a runner‑runner diamond flush possibility (very unlikely ~4–5%), and I block a lot of the Button’s Ax value combos. Calling here gives me ~30% pot‑odds (450 into a 1050 pot) which is in line with my equity vs a wide BTN c‑bet range; raising OOP would bloat the pot with a marginal hand and folding is too weak given the price. Plan: see the turn, fold to heavy turn aggression unless I pair the Ace or pick up a real draw; if I hit the Ace or a diamond I’ll switch to a more aggressive line.

Turn: the Qd gives me a real semi‑bluff — I now have A♦6♦ plus T♦ and Q♦ on board (4 diamonds), so I have 9 diamond outs and 3 remaining aces = 12 outs ≈ 26% to improve on the river. Elon checked to me after c‑betting the flop; that check can be weakness (missed Broadway/overcards) or a trap with a queen. Against a wide BTN c‑betting range, betting here is correct: it applies pressure to missed hands, preserves fold equity and still leaves me substantial equity if called. I’ll make a roughly half‑pot bet (750 into a 1500 pot) — big enough to fold out many unimproved hands but small enough to avoid overcommitting with only a draw. If he calls I go to the river with ~26% to improve; if he check‑raises small I can consider a call given my equity, but I’ll respect a large, polarized check‑raise (likely trips/full house) and fold. Overall: semi‑bluff now that I’ve picked up a real, nut‑oriented draw and hold the Ace blocker.

Full house 8s full of Queens (888QQ), an absolute monster on this Td8sQcQd board, crushing nearly all of Minni's range including top pair Qx, two pair QT, trips Qxx, weaker full houses like Q88 (Q full of 8s, which loses), J9 straight, etc. Only loses to pocket QQ (quads). Preflop she called my raise from BB with wide range per history (defends wide but folds to aggression often). Checked flop weak, called my value bet 450, now leads turn 750 after Q pairs, likely improved Qx or bluff/draw, but still behind. Pot 2250, call 750 (great odds ~4:1 effective), but she has only 1490 left (~15BB). Raising all-in on her (to 2240 total) maximizes value: denies her equity on river, extracts her stack now from worse (which she stations into per history of calling down light e.g., Hands 6,9,17,44), and has fold equity vs marginal hands like trips Q or two pair that can't call off jam. Calling risks free river card or her folding to river bet; flat suboptimal. Folding insane. Deep stack covers it easily.

I opened the turn as a semi‑bluff with A♦6♦ because the Qd gave me the nut‑flush draw plus an Ace blocker. After my 750 lead Elon’s huge check‑raise to 2,240 (pot now 4,490; I must call 1,490 — all‑in) is massively polarized and represents a very strong value range (trip/boat/quads or a set/overpair). Calling 1,490 into a 4,490 pot gives me ~25% required equity (1,490 / (4,490+1,490) ≈ 24.9%). My raw outs are 9 diamonds + 3 aces = 12 outs ≈ 26% (12/46), but many of those outs aren’t clean vs the hands Elon is signaling — if he has a queen or already a full house/quads my ace-outs don’t help and some river cards will give him a full house. Given the size of the raise, his line looks value‑heavy; my realistic equity is below the required threshold and calling would commit my entire stack in a marginal, high‑variance spot. I’ll fold and preserve chips to wait for a better pricing spot to get involved.

Heads-up on the button (SB) with A♣7♦. Stack is 1,440 (~14.4 BB) and the pot is 150 with 50 to call. In heads-up play A7o is a clear raising hand — limp/call is too passive and gives the big blind a cheap chance to see a flop with a wide range. With a short-to-medium stack (~14 BB) I want initiative and fold equity but also some postflop maneuvering room, so I’ll open to the legal minimum (200 total). That puts pressure on the BB, keeps the pot controlled, and leaves ~1,240 behind to play postflop. Plan if I’m called: continuation-bet on favorable A-high or dry boards, pot-control and check back or fold to heavy aggression on very coordinated boards. If Elon 3-bets large (committing most of my stack), I’ll fold A7o rather than getting it in dominated; if he 3-bets to a small size I can consider calling.

9d4s is a complete trash hand offsuit, gapped, no high card value. Facing a button/SB raise to 200 (2x), pot 300, calling 100 OOP. Terrible equity vs any reasonable opening range, poor postflop playability, no fold equity if raising. Minni short-stacked at 1290 but doesn't change the fundamentals. Standard fold to preserve stack.

Heads-up on the button with Q9o, a strong raising hand in position. Minni is short-stacked at ~15BB (1490 chips), making this an ideal spot to steal the blinds or get value if she shoves light. Q9o has good equity against typical shoving ranges and plays well postflop if called. Raising to 3x (300 total) applies pressure without overcommitting given the deep stacks disparity.

Heads-up in the big blind with K♣J♥ vs a 3x button open: this is a clear defend. The pot is 400 and I only need to put in 200 more (300 total including my posted 100) for good pot odds to see a flop. With my stack ~14.9BB I don’t want to commit too many chips preflop with a hand that plays well post-flop; a small raise to 500 has little extra fold equity versus an aggressive, deep-stacked opener and shoving is over-committing. I’ll call to realize equity, see a flop, and play post-flop for value or pot control depending on texture.

Heads‑up vs a button 3x open — I defended preflop with K♣J♥ which is reasonable. Flop 6♠5♥4♦ is very connected to the opener’s range (small pairs, 4/5/6, straight draws) and my hand has no pair, only two overcards and a weak backdoor heart. Pot = 600, my stack = 1290 (SPR ≈ 2.1) so a large c‑bet will effectively commit me with marginal equity. I want pot control and to see how Elon reacts — check to induce a c‑bet and call small/standard bets to realize equity or fold to heavy pressure. If I pair a K/J on later streets I can extract value; otherwise avoid bluff‑raising into a huge stack.

Preflop raise got called by short-stacked Minni (13BB effective). Flop 6s5h4d is highly coordinated with straight draws (3-7, wheel draws), pairs possible. Minni checks, giving me initiative. Q9o has Queen-high overcards (blocks some Qx), backdoor spade flush draw, and decent equity vs her likely calling range (pairs below Q, draws like 78, 37, A3 etc.). As preflop aggressor in position with massive stack advantage, c-bet for thin value from worse (overpairs unlikely, smaller pairs/draws call), protection, and huge fold equity (she can't call off 13BB lightly without top pair+ or strong draw). 2/3 pot size (400) balances value/protection/deny equity while keeping pot manageable if shoved on.

Heads‑up vs a 3x button open — I defended preflop with KcJh and the flop 6♠5♥4♦ is very connected to the opener’s range (small pairs, 4/5/6 combos, straight draws), but the button also c‑bets a lot of air. I have two overcards with ~6 immediate outs (~24% to pair by river) and reasonable equity against a wide c‑betting range. Facing 400 into a 1000 pot, calling keeps the pot under control, realizes my equity, and lets me fold to heavy turn aggression if needed. Raising to the min (800) would overcommit with a marginal hand given my SPR and the opponent’s depth. Plan: call the flop, re‑evaluate on the turn — extract value if I pair a K/J, fold to significant turn pressure if I miss.

Turn: I’ve just paired the J (Jh) — I hold top pair (Jacks) with a King kicker. Elon opened wide from the button and c‑bet the connected 6–5–4 flop, so his range contains lots of air/overcards, single pairs and some draws; many of those hands are now behind my hand. With pot = 1,400 and an effective stack of 890, I should extract value and deny free rivers to straight/runner draws rather than check‑back and give him a cheap card. A ~50% pot bet balances getting called by worse Jx / KJ / some AJs and charging draws, while not overcommitting if he raises big (which would typically represent two‑pair/sets). Plan: bet for value ~700; if he calls I’ll re‑evaluate the river, if he makes a large polarized raise I’ll assume stronger two‑pair/set and fold.

Facing a turn lead of 700 into 2100 pot (~33% pot) from short-stacked Minni (190 behind, ~2BB) after she check-called flop c-bet on highly coordinated 654 board. My Qd9s is queen-high with a weak spade blocker but no pair, no straight draw, no flush draw (only one spade, board two spades). Js turn completes spades FD, some straight draws (e.g., 78 already had straight on flop, possible Jx pairs). Her check-call-flop then lead-turn polarizes to strong made hands (Jx+, sets, straights like 78/23) or nut draws (spade FDs like AKs/QTs suited spades). Q9 blocks QJ but poor equity (~20-30% at best vs her strong polarized range). Pot odds require ~23% equity, marginal at best, and calling commits ~1/3 pot with reverse implied odds given her tiny remaining stack (she'll likely shove river). Raising has poor fold equity vs committed shorty and worse SPR post. Preserve massive stack advantage (37k vs her ~1k effective); folding high card no equity is standard GTO vs aggressive shortstack line.

Heads-up on the button (I’m the SB) with Kh Jh — a strong, suited broadway in position and ~22.4 BB effective (stack 2240). The pot is 150 and I’ve already posted 50, so calling would be 100 total but I want initiative and to deny Elon cheap flops. Min raise to 200 is the standard, size-efficient open here: raise to 200 total (add 150) to get fold equity, keep a playable stack for postflop play in position, and pressure an opponent who has shown frequent aggression. If Elon 3-bets a large sizing I’ll re-evaluate (likely fold to extreme overbets, consider calling small 3-bets or shoving vs very small 3-bets), but my plan is to open to 200 and play the flop in position.

Preflop in the big blind facing a button min-raise to 200 (100 to call into 300 pot). My hand 3h6s is trash: low, offsuit, gapped connectors at best but poor playability. Minni's stack is short at 2090 (~21BB effective), but my equity against her wide button opening range is poor (~30% or less tops), and I gain no fold equity worth chasing with this hand out of position. Standard fold to preserve stack and wait for better spots with 376 deep stacks left and 26 hands to go. No history suggests Minni is super loose-aggressive here to justify a speculative call.

Pocket 9s is a premium hand heads-up, strong enough to raise first in from the SB/Button for value and to build the pot while taking initiative. We have position postflop, and Minni's short stack (about 23BB) means we can apply pressure. Standard raise size around 3x the BB to 300 total. Min raise is 200, but 300 is appropriate for value.

I'm heads-up in the big blind with K♦Q♦ (Kd Qh) and Elon (SB) opened to 300. KQo is a strong heads-up hand and I don't want to play a marginal pot out of position; calling 200 is cheap but gives the button initiative postflop. With ~23bb effective I prefer to 3-bet to seize initiative and get fold equity. Min 3-bet is 500, I'll make a standard 3x-sized 3-bet to 900 — I add 800 more chips now (pot becomes ~1200); if he calls he puts in 600 more and the pot will be ~1800, leaving me ~1490 behind to maneuver. This size balances fold equity and postflop playability against an aggressive, wide opener, so: raise to 900.

Pocket 9s remains very strong heads-up against a short-stacked opponent's 3-bet. Minni has ~15BB effective, making her range polarized: premiums (JJ+, AK) or bluffs/shoves. Folding is too tight preflop in position. Raising all-in overkill with deep stack and fold equity unnecessary given pot odds (calling 600 into 1200, ~3:1 implied). Calling commits 600 to a 1800 pot, and if she jams her remaining 1490, odds improve to ~2.2:1 direct + massive implied. Position allows control postflop; can check-raise strong boards or bet folds. Her history shows aggression but frequent folds to pressure, so exploitable. Maximize EV by calling.

Stack/pot math and board texture: pot is 1,800 and I have 1,490 left (~14.9BB effective). Flop is 9♥ 8♦ 7♥. With K♦Q♥ I have two overcards and only a runner‑runner heart flush possibility (very unlikely). The flop heavily favors Elon’s calling range to my 3‑bet (pocket pairs, 9/8/7 combos, suited connectors and straight draws) — many of those hands have already connected. Shoving here for fold equity would require him to fold a lot of hands, but given he called a 3‑bet he will call my jam with a wide portion of his range (pairs and good draws), so my fold equity is thin and calling range equity is often poor. A small c‑bet just bloats the pot and leaves me vulnerable to a raise I can’t comfortably continue against. Best play is pot control: check and see how he responds. If he checks back I get a free turn to possibly pair up; if he bets small I can call some of the time and re‑evaluate on the turn; if he barrels large I can fold and preserve my remaining stack for a better spot. Therefore: check.

Top set on a draw-heavy flop (9h8d7h): open-enders (T6,JT), flush draw, combos. Crushes villain's 3-bet range (JJ+, TT/88 unlikely post-check, AJs/KQs, bluffs). Her check likely weakness (no strong made hand like JJ plays slow sometimes, but rare trap). Deep stack vs her 1490 (~15BB), bet for value/protection/denial. Size 2/3 pot (1200) extracts from overpairs (AA/KK/JJ), floats, draws; sets up all-in (she shoves 290 more, we snap). Pot-committing her maximizes EV as we never fold. History shows Minni calls wide but folds to pressure often. Position key for river control if check-fold.

I checked the flop to control the pot with K♦Q♥. Elon’s 1,200 into the 1,800 pot makes the pot 3,000 and I’m getting ~28.6% break‑even (1,200 / 4,200) to call. Versus a cold‑call then c‑bet range (broadways, suited connectors and some pairs) KQ has meaningful equity — two overcards, a queen blocker and occasional showdown value — so calling to realize that equity is correct. Shoving to 1,490 (all‑in) buys almost no fold equity because he only has to call an extra 290 and will call with most hands that already called my 3‑bet (pairs and strong draws). Folding here surrenders too much expected value. I will call 1,200, keep ~290 behind, and re‑evaluate on the turn (shove for value if I pair up or fold to heavy continued aggression if the turn closes out my outs).

Pot is 4,200 and I have only 290 left (very short). Board 9♥‑8♦‑7♥‑9♣ pairs the 9 — Elon’s line (cold‑call 3‑bet then a flop barrel) is weighted toward 9x, connected hands and pairs; many of those now beat me (trip 9s, two‑pair, strong pairs). I have only ~6 clean outs to pair (≈13% on the river) and no realistic fold equity: shoving 290 into a 4,200 pot is a tiny price for Elon to call and he will call with most of his continuing range. Betting small as a bluff/block is also ineffective — it won’t force enough folds and leaves me in a bad-spotted call. Best play is pot control: check and see the river. If Elon checks back I keep my equity; if he bets large I will fold because I’m almost certainly behind. Therefore: check.

I hold pocket 9s on a board of 9h 8d 7h 9c, giving me quads, the absolute nuts. The turn paired the 9s, improving me from trips to quads. Minni is extremely short with only 290 chips left behind into a 4200 pot. She checked the turn, which is weak but with pot odds over 14:1, she'll call any reasonable hand or draw. History shows Minni calls wide when short. Betting her full stack extracts maximum value without giving her a free river card. No need to check as there's no future street risk and we want her chips now. Position and deep stack irrelevant here; just get it in.

Pot is 4,490 and I must call 290 (which is my entire remaining stack). Break‑even on this call is 290 / (4,490 + 290) ≈ 6.1%. With K♦Q♥ on 9♥‑8♦‑7♥‑9♣ I have 6 clean outs (3 kings + 3 queens) to pair on the river, ≈6/46 ≈ 13.0% equity to improve. There is no way for me to make a flush (only two hearts on board + one in my hand leaves at most four hearts after the river), and I have no straight outs. Even allowing that some outs can be non‑clean (opponent may hold a king/queen or already have trips), my raw equity comfortably exceeds the tiny price I’m being offered. Folding here surrenders a +EV opportunity; raising isn’t realistic with my stack. Plan: call the 290 all‑in to realize those outs and force a river decision — if I miss the river I’ll fold to heavy action, if I pair up (especially a K) I’ll frequently have the best hand and can proceed accordingly.
